sql_array: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(50), account_type VARCHAR(20));
INSERT INTO users (id, username, account_type) VALUES (1, 'user1', 'basic');
INSERT INTO users (id, username, account_type) VALUES (2, 'user2', 'admin');
INSERT INTO users (id, username, account_type) VALUES (3, 'user3', NULL);
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM users) AND account_type IS NOT NULL;
SELECT username, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY account_type) as type_count FROM users HAVING type_count > 1;
SELECT username FROM users WHERE account_type IN (SELECT DISTINCT account_type FROM users WHERE username LIKE 'user%');
SELECT username FROM users WHERE username IS NOT NULL OR (select COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE username IS NULL) > 0;
SELECT username || ' is a ' || (CASE WHEN account_type IS NOT NULL THEN account_type ELSE 'unknown' END) as user_info FROM users;
SELECT * FROM users WHERE account_type = 'admin' AND id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = 'user2');
CREATE TABLE user_logs (user_id INT, action VARCHAR(50), log_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO user_logs (user_id, action) VALUES ((SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = 'user1'), 'login');
SELECT * FROM user_logs WHERE log_time >= (SELECT MIN(log_time) FROM user_logs WHERE user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = 'user2'));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE account_type IS NULL OR (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) = 0;
SELECT * FROM users WHERE username IN (SELECT DISTINCT username FROM users WHERE LENGTH(username) = 0) OR account_type = 'basic';
SELECT * WHERE { ?s a <AdminUser> } UNION SELECT * WHERE { ?s a <RegularUser> };
